119229.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When an issue plays a big role, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to make a decision. This engages people in local democracy. In Middelburg, we have recently worked on the citizen assembly.

119232.explanation: We consider it important that people living in the city center can continue to do so comfortably. Opening and closing times allow us to make good arrangements with police and community officers about safety.

119237.explanation: Clean air is essential for health. Therefore, we promote clean air in our municipality for better health of our residents.

119239.explanation: The best New Year's celebration is one we celebrate together, with a joint countdown and a light show on the market. Fireworks are not required; what matters more is celebrating the new year together. This is also stated in the advisory report of the citizen assembly (2025).

119241.explanation: We see the importance of (more) events in our municipality, but the balance with livability is very important. Therefore, we want (more) control over sound levels and enforce end times.

119242.explanation: Accessibility is vital for people with disabilities. However, many important places are not yet properly accessible, for example for wheelchair users. Entrepreneurs often cannot afford such adaptations on their own. Therefore, we believe the municipality should provide support.

119246.explanation: Children are under huge pressure, from addictive social media to performance pressure at school. Music is the perfect outlet. We want music lessons not only to be accessible to children with wealthy parents. This way, we offer every child equal opportunities.

119247.explanation: Sports provide lots of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy, and often the sports club is also a meeting place. If a sports club collapses, an entire neighborhood suffers, so we believe the municipality should intervene if necessary.

119249.explanation: We invest in neighborhood officers and community officers. Together with them, we strengthen cooperation with care, welfare, schools, and housing corporations.

119250.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But we do not believe more cameras are the right solution. Your privacy is a great value that must not be lightly infringed. Therefore, we prefer more enforcement officers on the street who can also intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

119252.explanation: Community officers must be approachable. They seek cooperation with care, welfare, education, and housing corporations. A baton does not help with this.

119257.explanation: To realize more homes, the municipality can provide subsidies or purchase properties itself. For example, the old police station in Middelburg was purchased with the aim of converting it into apartments.

119259.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

119260.explanation: If debt forgiveness of municipal taxes stops, people with the lowest incomes in our municipality will get into big trouble. They simply cannot miss that money. We believe everyone should be able to keep their head above water, which is why we support debt forgiveness.

119263.explanation: There must be more parking spaces where electric cars can be charged, but this should not come at the expense of the available 'regular' spaces. This can be done either by creating extra spaces or by allowing other cars to use charging spaces as well.

119265.explanation: The neighborhoods outside the city center should not become permit zones, at most areas with parking discs (blue zones). To prevent visitors from parking in the neighborhoods, there must be sufficient parking facilities within walking distance at the edges.

119267.explanation: We consider freedom of choice very important, also when choosing healthcare providers.

119269.explanation: Menstrual products are basic necessities. Fortunately, in Middelburg they are already being distributed at more and more locations, such as the library, the food bank, and in education.

119273.explanation: Middelburg has limited space for business growth. If we have to choose, we prefer to use the land in our municipality for residential growth, our energy transition, and space for cultural, sports, and other facilities.

119274.explanation: In 2025, together with several other parties, we ensured that all shops are allowed to open on Sundays. We believe it is important that shops can be open on Sundays for both retailers and customers who want it.

119275.explanation: For quick integration, to reduce pressure on asylum reception centers (AZCs), and to allow municipalities to fulfill their legal housing duties, the priority regulation is necessary. Municipalities must ensure there is sufficient housing for all target groups.

119278.explanation: We believe the municipality should consult with the new operator to implement innovations in the service offering in the coming years, and also take steps towards making the building more sustainable.

119281.explanation: We are not opposed in principle to better controlling soft drug use via a coffeeshop, but we believe that the area around Middelburg already has enough coffeeshops to meet this need.

119283.explanation: Our ambitions for housing construction in Middelburg are high; we are also not opposed to high-rise buildings where they fit in the surroundings, as this should certainly be considered an option.

119286.explanation: There should be fewer parking spaces for visitors of the city center, and these spaces should be realized at the edges of Middelburg. The parking spots in the center of Middelburg should be used much more for the residents of the city center.

119288.explanation: By legally allowing sex businesses, we hope to combat human trafficking, exploitation, and illegal home prostitution. Of course, when establishing a legal sex business, attention must be paid to livability in the street and neighborhood.

119289.explanation: Our ambitions for housing construction in Middelburg are high; we are also not afraid to jump over the N57 highway, and where this fits into the surroundings, this should certainly be considered an option.

119290.explanation: The municipality of Middelburg is currently already spending money on the restoration of the windmill De Hoop, which we are very pleased about.

119229.explanation: A referendum is black and white. Participation provides a much broader picture. Residents can express an opinion then. That is always possible with the LPM.

119232.explanation: Yes, but with conditions. Not everywhere, and with measures so that no nuisance occurs.

119237.explanation: We will provide good information about clean burning and under which weather conditions nuisance can occur.

119239.explanation: Now that private fireworks are no longer allowed, the municipality can protect the tradition.

119241.explanation: However, we must counteract nuisance as much as possible.

119242.explanation: This could be in the form of a loan, for example.

119246.explanation: Every child should be able to take music lessons. We only support families where that is necessary. We also contribute to the music school.

119247.explanation: Sports are important. However, we must look for smart solutions, such as cooperation, so that the municipality does not have to provide permanent support.

119249.explanation: The extra BOAs (special enforcement officers) we have already added are still insufficient to provide good support and enforcement in all neighborhoods.

119250.explanation: Camera surveillance, for example at the Vlasmarkt and near Tref, could help with safety. Privacy is important, so we must be cautious.

119252.explanation: We can only equip BOAs with batons if they have had proper training.

119257.explanation: When it comes to social rental housing.

119259.explanation: If possible, we guide residents towards (paid) work.

119260.explanation: Everything is becoming more expensive. The municipality can help here.

119263.explanation: If residents need charging stations, the municipality must provide them. There must be sufficient parking spaces for all residents.

119265.explanation: Parking permits should only be introduced where residents find them necessary.

119267.explanation: Care should be appropriate but also affordable.

119269.explanation: This must be part of our poverty policy.

119270.explanation: If the property tax (OZB) can sustainably and structurally be lowered, we should do that.

119272.explanation: Tourists should also contribute to the municipality, not just residents.

119273.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to do business; we can start by finding space on existing sites. Industry belongs in Vlissingen-East.

119274.explanation: Thanks to the LPM, shops are allowed to be open every Sunday. We want to maintain that.

119278.explanation: The swimming pool is not very old yet. However, we must ensure good management and therefore maintenance.

119281.explanation: Only if there is no nuisance.

119283.explanation: Yes, in places where it fits in the environment, such as along the canal.

119286.explanation: First, we must ensure there is enough space on the edge of the city center.

119288.explanation: A legal business is much better than illegal prostitution.

119289.explanation: Many new homes must be built; on the east side of the N57 there is enough space.

119229.explanation: The elected council is there to make decisions. Good consultation works better than expensive voting rounds.

119232.explanation: Disagree: Peace is also a right. No nighttime pub crawls in Middelburg.

119237.explanation: It's allowed, but only if neighbors are considerate of each other and stove users consider the weather and the quality of the wood. Cosiness is possible, but without nuisance and smog on the street.

119239.explanation: Organizing a fireworks show? That is not a core task of the municipality. Let residents organize a festive New Year's Eve that is safe and respectful of each other and the environment.

119241.explanation: Stricter noise standards help limit nuisance and maintain peace. This keeps room for activities without windows shaking and residents losing their night's rest.

119242.explanation: Accessibility is important, but entrepreneurs may open their doors wide themselves. The municipality can help with advice, not with money. This keeps burdens light and thresholds low.

119246.explanation: Music makes life more beautiful, but it is no reason for a municipal money tap. We support culture, not a standard subsidy for every child.

119247.explanation: Sport is healthy, but no municipal lifebuoy. Clubs must stand strong on their own; we help with conditions.

119249.explanation: In favor: More BOAs? That is already arranged. Safety is important, but we won’t keep increasing until it becomes a BOA parade. First properly deploy the current reinforcements.

119250.explanation: In favor: Camera surveillance can help keep Middelburg safe, but not Big Brother. Use it smartly where it is really needed, not on every street corner.

119252.explanation: Safety is important, but a baton does not make a neighborhood pleasant. First focus on visibility and approachability. If working conditions make it necessary in the future, these tools must be considered carefully. Not a hard yes or no, but a balanced approach.

119257.explanation: Neither: Converting vacant offices? Fine idea, but let market parties take the lead. The municipality doesn't need to open a subsidy fund.

119259.explanation: Welfare is not a hammock. Those who can must contribute with socially useful work. This way we help each other and keep Middelburg moving.

119260.explanation: Disagree: Stop debt forgiveness? No! For those with little, every euro counts. We remain supportive and ensure burdens do not weigh unnecessarily.

119263.explanation: Agree: Electric driving requires electricity, not stress. More parking spaces as charging spots? Yes please, this keeps Middelburg moving and green.

119265.explanation: Disagree: Parking permits everywhere? That is too strict. Only implement where nuisance really occurs, so it remains fair and practical.

119267.explanation: Agree: Care is more than help; it is also trust. Everyone must be able to choose a caregiver that fits their faith, culture, or conviction.

119269.explanation: Disagree: Free tampons and sanitary pads? No, the municipality prefers to help via existing minimum income policies and support, not free products.

119270.explanation: Agree: Lower property tax? Yes please! Taxes must remain as low as possible so living in Middelburg remains affordable.

119272.explanation: Neither: Increase or decrease tourist tax? Not necessary. We'll keep it stable and mainly ensure tourism does not flood Middelburg.

119273.explanation: Agree: More business parks? Yes, but smart and sustainable. This keeps Middelburg economically strong and livable.

119274.explanation: Definitely restrict: More shopping Sundays? Absolutely not! Sunday remains a day of rest, not shopping. Middelburg chooses quiet over retail crowds.

119275.explanation: No priority for social housing? No. Everyone deserves a fair chance, even with scarcity. Equal treatment keeps Middelburg balanced.

119278.explanation: Neither: Renew the swimming pool or not? The program doesn't address this. The focus is on sport in general, not one pool.

119281.explanation: Disagree: Coffeeshop in Middelburg? No, we keep it sober. No room for soft drugs, but for a healthy and safe living environment.

119283.explanation: More high-rise buildings? Yes, but with policy! A good urban plan and no higher than 7 floors, so Middelburg remains stylish and future-proof.

119286.explanation: Disagree: Fewer parking spaces? No, the number remains the same. But move them to the edges so residents keep space and tourists park neatly outside the city center.

119288.explanation: A sex business in Middelburg? No, we choose a safe and healthy city. No normalization of prostitution, but protection of norms and values.

119289.explanation: Agree: Jump over the N57? Yes! A new residential area along the canal provides space for growth but with attention to quality and accessibility.

119290.explanation: We have already allocated money for the mill, but not for a full restoration. It is about a careful balance between cultural preservation and other priorities, not black-and-white choices.

119229.explanation: Besides representation, participation provides a good picture.

119232.explanation: Entrepreneurs receive freedom to do business and develop new ideas. The municipality coordinates and supports. They set rules regarding opening hours.

119237.explanation: The municipality joins the Clean Air Agreement. Burning wood in residential areas is restricted to reduce nuisance.

119239.explanation: From the citizen council in 2025, a central, communal countdown moment was desired. Possibly with a light show.

119241.explanation: Noise standards must be enforced. Festivals and parties are a nice addition to the city's activities.

119242.explanation: Primarily, public spaces must be accessible to people with disabilities. The municipality ensures this.

119246.explanation: We support music education because it is important.

119247.explanation: Sports clubs contribute to a healthy lifestyle and a healthy society. Therefore, they are optimally supported in their implementation.

119249.explanation: Safety promotes the livability for all residents.

119250.explanation: Camera surveillance, with the necessary (privacy) safeguards, is a tool for prevention.

119252.explanation: In our view, there should be more enforcement officers engaged in enforcement and visible in the neighborhood. They must be able to optimally access the necessary means of force if needed.

119257.explanation: Cooperation can be granted to initiatives. Every year, subsidy opportunities are offered by the Province of Zeeland. (Reuse of vacant buildings)

119259.explanation: Receiving benefits means social participation, for example through volunteer work.

119260.explanation: Each year it must be assessed whether there is sufficient payment capacity. For remission over multiple consecutive years, automatic remission can be granted.

119263.explanation: The CDA believes that a good parking policy should include sufficient charging facilities.

119265.explanation: When a parking problem arises, first consideration can also be given to a blue zone.

119267.explanation: If this is practically feasible, it is an option.

119270.explanation: The municipal budget is structurally balanced and contains sufficient reserves to absorb (structural) setbacks.

119272.explanation: Middelburg is hospitable. The tourist tax will not be raised further in the coming council period.

119273.explanation: Collaboration on Walcheren is being further inventoried. When opportunities arise, this is also possible within the municipality of Middelburg.

119274.explanation: Stores throughout the municipality may already be open on all Sundays and public holidays between 6:00 a.m. and 10:00 p.m.

119275.explanation: Based on regional agreements, the municipality will ensure a solution.

119278.explanation: The municipalities of Middelburg and Vlissingen must jointly maintain an urban regional swimming pool with minimal financial risks.

119281.explanation: The CDA does not want a coffeeshop in Middelburg.

119283.explanation: Efficient use of building land is desirable. High-rise buildings must not disturb the skyline of the current buildings.

119286.explanation: Parking spaces may only be removed when new ones have been realized outside the center.

119288.explanation: It is currently possible to apply for an operating permit for this. There is no reason to make such an application impossible.

119289.explanation: A new residential area can be realized there when the growing demand can no longer be met by expanding existing neighborhoods.

119290.explanation: When various financial streams make the desired restoration possible, the municipality of Middelburg can contribute to this.

119229.explanation: D66 Middelburg is a champion of citizen participation. Whether that happens through a citizens' assembly, a consultation evening, or indeed a referendum depends on the subject.

119232.explanation: As far as D66 Middelburg is concerned, hospitality in Middelburg may have more freedom regarding opening hours. That does not necessarily mean fully deregulating opening hours immediately.

119237.explanation: D66 Middelburg believes Middelburg should also sign and implement the Clean Air Agreement (SLA). Emission of fine dust from wood burning belongs to this. We comply with national laws and regulations. A complete ban is, for now, too far.

119239.explanation: The New Year's celebration should once again be a festival for everyone, free from the increasing violence of recent years. If the national fireworks ban comes into effect, D66 thinks it's a good idea for the municipality to organize an alternative show.

119241.explanation: Noise nuisance from events usually does not come from the applied norms but from exceeding them. This must be strictly enforced. Further tightening of the norms does not directly contribute.

119242.explanation: The municipality of Middelburg must implement the UN Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities. Subsidies may be available to make private buildings more accessible. D66 Middelburg believes the entrepreneur also has personal responsibility here.

119246.explanation: Every child must be able to participate in sports, games, and music education. Through the sports and culture fund, we make this possible in Middelburg, also for low-income families. D66 Middelburg wants to continue this.

119247.explanation: Sports clubs play an important social role. Where needed, for example with new facilities or sustainability improvements, we look at whether the municipality can help.

119249.explanation: Enforcement officers (BOA's) play an important role in keeping our municipality safe and making people feel safe.

119250.explanation: Camera surveillance can be a last resort to restore peace and safety in an area. D66 Middelburg is cautious about this due to privacy concerns.

119252.explanation: At present, there appears to be no reason for this in Middelburg. Arming enforcement officers requires a lot from the officers themselves. This is not our preference.

119257.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes with municipal subsidies addresses the housing shortage, prevents deterioration, and uses existing buildings sustainably. This way, we work on affordable housing with less environmental impact and can preserve our outdoor space.

119259.explanation: The role of the municipality is mainly to help people re-enter the regular labor market. Social work can assist with this.

119260.explanation: Remission schemes exist for a good reason. D66 Middelburg

119263.explanation: Residents can apply themselves for a public charging point. This system works well and, according to D66 Middelburg, we will continue it.

119265.explanation: Permitted parking should, for now, remain limited to the city center according to D66. However, expanding blue zones can be considered if increasing parking pressure gives cause.

119267.explanation: Being able to choose the healthcare provider is a great good for D66 Middelburg. If this free choice leads to extra costs, these may be charged to the applicant.

119269.explanation: The municipality can support initiatives to tackle period poverty. As far as D66 is concerned, the municipality should not actively distribute tampons or sanitary pads itself.

119270.explanation: D66 Middelburg stands for sound financial policy. In our opinion, the property tax should not be raised beyond the inflation correction.

119272.explanation: Middelburg is a beautiful, monumental city. Visitors contribute through the tourist tax to keeping our municipality clean, beautiful, and safe. D66 Middelburg believes the tourist tax should rise in line with the municipal charges for residents.

119273.explanation: The municipality of Middelburg should restructure existing industrial areas and allocate existing space. Only afterwards can it be considered whether there is still a need for new industrial sites.

119274.explanation: Sunday opening hours were already regulated during the past council period. Shops may decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays. We are pleased with this and will continue accordingly.

119275.explanation: Asylum seekers with a residence permit should integrate into society as quickly as possible. These people usually lack a social network where they can find shelter.

119278.explanation: The swimming pool is an important facility for Middelburg and Vlissingen. A well-equipped swimming pool is important for swimming education but also vital for adult swimming, especially for the elderly.

119281.explanation: The establishment of a coffeeshop can contribute to better regulation, less nuisance, and increased safety. Through supervision and clear rules, control arises along with a responsible offer for users.

119283.explanation: To address the housing shortage on our limited space, it is only logical that we build upwards.

119286.explanation: By creating more parking spaces at the edge of the city, parking spots in the city center can eventually be removed. This way, we work towards a car-reduced city center.

119288.explanation: This has already been done by making sex work subject to permits. We prefer it to happen legally and openly rather than underground.

119289.explanation: The housing shortage is enormous, including in Middelburg. To meet the demand for new homes, D66 Middelburg wants to take the leap over N57.

119290.explanation: D66 Middelburg has always supported the citizen initiative to restore the windmill De Hoop. We will continue to do so.

119229.explanation: There are sufficient opportunities for residents to participate, such as citizen assemblies, input in faction and committee meetings, and of course the elections.

119232.explanation: For the livability of the city, the ChristenUnie chooses caution and customization. The General Local By-law (APV) provides exemptions that entrepreneurs can apply for in specific situations, where the municipality weighs the various interests.

119237.explanation: The ChristenUnie is concerned about the nuisance and sometimes serious harm caused by wood burning, especially for people with vulnerable health, and believes the municipality should proactively inform and advise. However, a ban is unenforceable and national regulations are lacking.

119239.explanation: One of the recommendations from the citizen assembly is to organize a joint countdown moment. A fireworks show fits well with this advice.

119241.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers it important that there is room for music festivals and events because they contribute to community spirit and social cohesion. It is important that existing standards are not exceeded and that enforcement takes place.

119242.explanation: Accessibility of public venues for people with disabilities is very important to us. Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for an accessible and customer-friendly setup.

119246.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes music education is very important for children's development. Music education not only provides enjoyment and relaxation, but children also develop creativity, self-confidence, and cognitive and socio-emotional skills, such as language, math skills, and cooperation.

119247.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health, social interaction, and community spirit. If they get into trouble due to unforeseen circumstances, support is justified. Investing in clubs is investing in prevention, volunteers, and a strong, connected local society.

119249.explanation: Due to staff shortages at the police and extra tasks for public order officers (BOAs), enforcement is under pressure. More BOAs can help but is not a miracle solution. Good prioritization and cooperation remain necessary, as it remains about allocating scarce capacity.

119250.explanation: Camera surveillance seems a good solution to guarantee safety, but there are drawbacks. It is allowed only for a limited period, the images cannot be used freely, and immediate reactions are not always possible. Moreover, the privacy of local residents requires adequate protection.

119252.explanation: The ChristenUnie prefers de-escalation. Arming can escalate situations and does not suit all BOA tasks. It also requires intensive training, strict rules, and supervision. We support proper training and cooperation with the police.

119257.explanation: Converting an office is complicated and cannot be solved by subsidy alone. Laws and regulations are often a stumbling block. According to the ChristenUnie, subsidies are appropriate only if they can make a difference and for plans with societal added value.

119259.explanation: This is laid down in the Participation Act. The ChristenUnie sees that, if done according to ability, based on trust and customized approach, it can have a positive effect. Meaningful engagement is good for people and societal involvement offers support and perspective.

119260.explanation: The ChristenUnie considers it unwise to (additionally) tax people with low incomes. Debt remission prevents problematic debts and contributes to subsistence security.

119263.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports sustainable mobility and CO₂ reduction. More charging points make electric driving more accessible and fit future-oriented policies, with attention to livability, spatial quality, affordability, and other sustainable transport modes like cycling and public transport.

119265.explanation: The ChristenUnie views the parking permit as a solution for a problem that causes new problems itself. Therefore, we will not implement it hastily.

119267.explanation: The ChristenUnie realizes that care provision is not neutral. Caregivers bring their own backgrounds and values. Especially for people in vulnerable positions, it is valuable to be able to choose a caregiver they identify with.

119269.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes basic needs should remain accessible to everyone. Free tampons and sanitary pads contribute to health, equality, and social participation, and help reduce financial barriers for vulnerable groups.

119270.explanation: Without concrete reason or motivation, the ChristenUnie will not lower the property tax (OZB). It means less income, so this can only happen together with expenditure adjustments. The municipal council can also take strong increases in property values into account in OZB rates.

119272.explanation: The tourist tax is increased annually by an inflation percentage. And the number of overnight stays is increasing. Considering tourism-related costs, this seems sufficient for now. Therefore, the ChristenUnie does not support an increase in the tourist tax.

119273.explanation: The ChristenUnie first opts for revitalizing existing industrial areas, partly facilitated by the municipality. Because of long development times, coordination with the Walcheren municipalities and the province is urgent and crucial for coherence in approach so that businesses have perspective.

119274.explanation: Retailers can already open their shops every Sunday, so allowing more shopping Sundays is not possible. For the ChristenUnie, a flourishing economy is a blessing that cannot continue without necessary rest. Therefore, we cherish one rest day per week.

119275.explanation: Housing allocation is customized: urgent seekers, urgent cases, and recognized refugees. Not only because the law requires it but also for good asylum throughput, we choose to give priority to refugees with residence permits.

119278.explanation: Only after research and consultation can the ChristenUnie consider renewing the swimming pool and the part Middelburg will bear. The Vrijburgbad opened on July 1, 2003, so some renewal by now is necessary.

119281.explanation: The ChristenUnie does not want a coffeeshop because we want to protect vulnerable youth. Coffeeshops increase health risks, nuisance, and addiction danger. We choose prevention, education, and a society without drugs where people can live safely and healthily.

119283.explanation: The ChristenUnie wants more and faster building, with two-thirds affordable and 30% social housing. If land is expensive or scarce, high-rise buildings can be a solution to build affordably or socially. Of course, this will involve the Building Height Vision established at the end of 2025.

119286.explanation: The municipality has previously decided to make the city center low-traffic. Once more parking options have been realized at the edges, fewer parking spaces will be needed in the center.

119288.explanation: The Christian Union does not want a sex business in Middelburg to protect vulnerable people, prevent exploitation, and maintain the livability of the city.

119289.explanation: The housing shortage in Middelburg is so severe that doing nothing is not an option. The Christian Union prefers infill development first but does not rule out expansion. A residential area east of the N57 may be necessary, provided it is done carefully, with attention to nature, accessibility, and support.

119290.explanation: In May 2023, the municipal council positively decided on the citizen initiative to restore the windmill De Hoop to a full-fledged mill. For the Christian Union, it is therefore logical that the municipality financially contributes to this.

119229.explanation: We have a representative democracy in the Netherlands. Through the representative role, residents can be sufficiently consulted.

119232.explanation: We believe in freedom of entrepreneurship.

119237.explanation: The government must be very cautious in decisions made behind the doors of our inhabitants.

119239.explanation: That really adds something to the festive experience.

119241.explanation: We should not invent solutions for problems that do not exist.

119242.explanation: That is the responsibility of the entrepreneur, not the government.

119247.explanation: We will assess that on a case-by-case basis. Currently, this statement is too general.

119249.explanation: Absolutely. Especially in well-known problem areas like TREF and the Vlasmarkt.

119259.explanation: If they are physically able. Routine and experience can help them find work more easily.

119263.explanation: The supply in Middelburg is already ample.

119265.explanation: Middelburg must also be a welcoming city for visitors. Without alternative parking spaces, this is unwise.

119267.explanation: We must curb healthcare costs. This will not help with that.

119269.explanation: To be considered for certain target groups.

119270.explanation: The VVD wants tax relief for residents. This is the right choice to make.

119272.explanation: We must cherish Middelburg as a popular city for visitors. The tourist tax is already high enough.

119273.explanation: That need exists. Middelburg must develop economically stronger. Additional business parks contribute to this.

119274.explanation: All shops are already allowed to open on Sundays. A good thing!

119275.explanation: Our residents often wait a long time on housing lists. It is unfair to give priority to refugees.

119281.explanation: Not under the current tolerance policy. The VVD now recognizes the problems with illegal trade. A solution must be found for this in cooperation with The Hague.

119288.explanation: We have already made that decision. However, under strict conditions.

119289.explanation: There is a need for more houses. Middelburg must show more ambition in building homes.

119290.explanation: Together with the residents of Sint Laurens, explore what is possible.

119229.explanation: The referendum must concern a clear choice.

119232.explanation: The rules for opening hours are included in the General Local Regulation (APV) and established by the municipal council. Opening hours should be reasonable. It is also important to take into account residents, entrepreneurs, and employees.

119237.explanation: The stove guide must be actively promoted. So do not burn if it is not allowed based on the stove guide. It must not cause nuisance to health (choice of materials to be burned and use of filters that operate sustainably and emit as little harmful emissions as possible).

119241.explanation: The noise standards can be less strict. Better enforcement of noise standards is needed.

119242.explanation: Care for our fellow human beings is also a task of the municipality.

119246.explanation: Music lessons should be offered at school again. Furthermore, there are arrangements for people with low incomes.

119247.explanation: The story should be more nuanced, depending on the financial policy of the association.

119249.explanation: That must be done by the police. And it must remain a police responsibility.

119250.explanation: Preserve privacy. Only where absolutely necessary.

119252.explanation: Safety is a task for the police and that should remain so.

119257.explanation: There is a great need for affordable housing. If buildings already exist, it is faster to convert them into housing than to build new ones. And it takes up less space.

119259.explanation: It is a right and people do not receive welfare benefits just like that. Because they are unable to work.

119260.explanation: No, forgiveness (of debts) is important if people cannot pay. Opportunities should be expanded.

119265.explanation: It brings extra costs for residents. The municipality must facilitate sufficient parking spaces outside the city with a shuttle service.

119269.explanation: Yes, or at least the municipality must organize that it is distributed.

119270.explanation: It is important for the financing of, among other things, social provisions.

119272.explanation: Many facilities are also partly in the interest of tourists.

119273.explanation: There is enough space for businesses in the municipality.

119274.explanation: It is not good for employees either. We do not need a 24/7 economy.

119275.explanation: Because a status holder did not participate in a waiting list and to relieve the asylum seeker centers (AZC’s).

119278.explanation: The municipality of Middelburg must provide a sustainable swimming pool that meets the (legal) requirements of today. Residents of Middelburg must be able to swim and there must be a location for school swimming. The swimming pool's energy system is outdated.

119281.explanation: We want to prevent additional nuisance; there is already nuisance caused by tourism, and research shows that tourism is still increasing, and we do not want to add to that. We also foresee too many parking and traffic problems.

119283.explanation: We are in favor of a new residential building up to 30 meters high in the outskirts. There is a need for sufficient housing and land is scarce and expensive; moreover, we want to preserve greenery.

119286.explanation: Agreed, but sufficient parking spaces must remain available for residents. We believe that more parking spaces with a shuttle service should be offered at the edges of Middelburg, for example at the new community church.

119288.explanation: We want to prevent illegal prostitution and protect sex workers.

119289.explanation: There is so much demand for housing that we think this is necessary. As much as possible with high-rise buildings up to 30 meters, taking sight lines into account.

119290.explanation: It has been demonstrated that there is much support among the residents in Sint Laurens; the municipality should enable restoration, provided the residents' group actively commits to restoration.

119229.explanation: A citizens' council is our preference.

119232.explanation: It is better to standardize closing times.

119237.explanation: It is difficult to prohibit. Follow the Stookwijzer guideline as much as possible.

119239.explanation: It is costly and does not lead to less fireworks throughout the municipality.

119241.explanation: It is strict and must be enforced.

119242.explanation: It is the entrepreneur's own matter. It seems like a social duty to us.

119246.explanation: Music lessons at school are our preference.

119247.explanation: Sports clubs already receive subsidies; perhaps it can be increased.

119249.explanation: BOAs (special enforcement officers) are already required to take over police tasks. In places where insecurity is experienced, BOAs must be mandatorily present. (TREF) Due to the many tasks, there is a need for expansion.

119250.explanation: Camera surveillance may offer an adequate solution for the violence in the city center.

119252.explanation: Especially for their own safety, given the increasing aggression.

119257.explanation: Ban vacancy. Incentive measures to convert offices into apartments can offer a solution. The municipality can provide support when issuing permits.

119260.explanation: No, this leads to undesirable situations.

119267.explanation: Asking is possible. In practice, it is often not feasible.

119269.explanation: There are other options for low-income earners.

119270.explanation: If the budget allows, our residents have the right to it.

119273.explanation: Research shows there is not really a demand for it.

119274.explanation: There is a policy that allows shopping on Sundays.

119275.explanation: Those who have been waiting longest have priority. At the last registration, there were 1,569 applicants for one social rental home!

119283.explanation: That should also be possible in Middelburg within the agreed high-rise vision. Tall apartment buildings on the designated plots.

119286.explanation: Only if more parking spaces are available elsewhere.

119288.explanation: We should not want this in Middelburg.

119289.explanation: We must utilize that possibility, given the severe housing shortage.

119290.explanation: We must contribute to the restoration.

119229.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports direct participation of residents. They want to introduce advisory referendums for important decisions.

119232.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes the municipality should be able to set opening hours to safeguard the interests of residents, entrepreneurs, and hospitality staff. Middelburg has generous opening hours and research shows all parties are fairly satisfied with the current rules.

119237.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to fully ban wood burning. Wood burning produces a lot of fine particulate matter and causes health problems for many residents.

119239.explanation: The Party for the Animals is against fireworks nuisance. They support a ban on consumer fireworks and want no fireworks at events—not even a central show—to protect people and animals. A light show without nuisance could be an alternative.

119241.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to better protect neighbors and animals against noise. Their program states that maximum sound limits must be clear at festivals and that noise nuisance is actively limited.

119242.explanation: The Party for the Animals strongly emphasizes accessibility. New buildings must be accessible to everyone by default, and the municipality takes a leading role in making existing buildings accessible.

119246.explanation: The Party for the Animals values broad development. They invest in sports, swimming lessons, culture, and music lessons for all children so that finances are no obstacle.

119247.explanation: The Party for the Animals stands for a strong local community. They promise to support associations and preserve community life; the municipality will step in to help clubs stay afloat in financial trouble.

119249.explanation: The Party for the Animals invests in a visible presence on the streets. They want to deploy more street workers, neighborhood parents, enforcement officers, and community police officers as the 'eyes and ears' in the neighborhood to detect problems early.

119250.explanation: The Party for the Animals is cautious about camera surveillance due to privacy concerns. They state that camera surveillance only provides false security and should only be used temporarily under strict conditions.

119252.explanation: The Party for the Animals is cautious about issuing batons. In case of threat of violence, the enforcement officer should call the police.

119257.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to use existing buildings more cleverly to combat the housing shortage. Empty offices will be converted into homes.

119259.explanation: The Party for the Animals opposes mandatory counter-performance. Voluntary work and informal care are supported but not made compulsory. The focus is on the benefit recipient, not on providing free labor.

119260.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to spare low-income earners. Waivers should be possible for people with an income up to 130% of the social minimum.

119263.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to facilitate the transition to clean transport. Middelburg will provide sufficient charging points powered by green electricity for electric vehicles, integrated into the streetscape.

119265.explanation: Making the city center car-restricted causes commuters and tourists to shift to neighborhoods around the center. The Party for the Animals believes residents must be able to park in their own neighborhood. A 'blue zone' with parking permits can help with this.

119267.explanation: The Party for the Animals values tailored care. Care providers and shelters must take into account the background, identity, and lifestyle of clients so that everyone feels safe and understood in care.

119269.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to combat care poverty. Menstrual products should be available free of charge in public buildings such as schools, libraries, and care centers.

119270.explanation: The Party for the Animals has not planned to reduce the property tax (ozb). The national government is giving municipalities too little money from 2026 ('ravine year'), but Middelburg continues to invest in people, animals, and nature. A reduction in property tax would undermine that care and ambitions.

119272.explanation: The Party for the Animals considers the tourist tax a useful levy to finance sustainability. They specify that the revenue must be used to limit the damage tourism causes to climate and nature.

119273.explanation: The Party for the Animals aims for restructuring instead of expansion. Outdated industrial sites must become circular. Polluting industrial sites should be converted as much as possible into residential areas and green spaces.

119274.explanation: Not applicable. Since 2024, shops are allowed to open on all Sundays.

119275.explanation: The Party for the Animals supports the current policy to house status holders. They want to prevent refugees from being set against other housing seekers by creating enough affordable homes but do not oppose priority for this group.

119278.explanation: The Party for the Animals believes everyone should be able to participate. Sports and swimming must also be possible for people with limited budgets. The municipality must make strict agreements with operators about affordability.

119281.explanation: The Party for the Animals will not prohibit the opening of a coffee shop. The party chooses a health-focused approach over repression. Therefore, Middelburg is open to trials with local, sustainable, and organic cannabis cultivation.

119283.explanation: A residential tower of 12 floors, like one possibly to be built in Mortiere, is far too tall. It disrupts the cityscape and alienates residents from each other. The Party for the Animals supports a human scale: a maximum of four floors.

119286.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants far fewer parking places. For commuters and tourists, parking will be available on the city outskirts, linked to small-scale public transport and shared bikes. Plenty of space for (electric) bicycles and pedestrians. Many more facilities will be created to park your bike safely.

119288.explanation: The Party for the Animals is committed to the safety of sex workers. The municipality ensures safe, legal, and hygienic workplaces. This way, the municipality makes sex workers accessible to aid workers.

119289.explanation: The Party for the Animals follows the 'housing ladder': first making smarter use of existing buildings by transforming, for example, shops and addressing vacancy. Then through densification and splitting of existing buildings. Only then does new construction on the city outskirts come into consideration.

119290.explanation: The mill and warehouse have monument status. If the costs – which exceed one million euros – are (partly) charged to the municipality, the building must have a public function after restoration.

119229.explanation: We want binding referenda so that residents can directly express their opinions on important political decisions.

119232.explanation: Entrepreneurs are perfectly capable of determining their own opening hours. Of course, noise standards and other regulations must be observed to keep nuisance within limits.

119237.explanation: Freedom in energy choice is important. We will never agree to a heating ban or the prohibition of wood stoves and other traditional heating methods. We maintain natural gas, wood stoves, and other heating without pressure or bans.

119239.explanation: Fireworks are part of New Year's Eve, and if citizens want, they may set off fireworks themselves; that is not a government task. We are therefore against a general fireworks ban.

119241.explanation: Stricter standards are not necessary; the current rules do need to be enforced.

119242.explanation: People with disabilities must be able to participate well in society. It is good if entrepreneurs want to make their buildings more accessible, and financial support can be helpful. It must remain feasible and affordable.

119246.explanation: Every child should be able to have music lessons, but customization is required and subsidies will not be necessary in all cases.

119247.explanation: Clubs are burdened with ever stricter rules, higher tax pressure, and the huge rise in energy bills. This must be addressed first. We support sports clubs and actively seek cooperation where needed.

119249.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers have important tasks, but we keep neighborhood officers focused on their core duties. We do not make municipal enforcement an 'alternative police' and prevent officers from receiving tasks or powers belonging to the police.

119250.explanation: We deploy cameras incidentally and temporarily at problem locations to prevent large-scale surveillance.

119252.explanation: Municipal enforcement officers have important tasks, but we keep neighborhood officers focused on their core duties. We do not make municipal enforcement an 'alternative police' and prevent officers from receiving tasks or powers belonging to the police.

119257.explanation: We support transforming vacant offices into homes but are not great supporters of subsidies. Removing unnecessary regulations achieves much more.

119259.explanation: Our preference is reintegration into paid work in the regular labor market and making paid work rewarding. People who cannot return to the labor market can possibly do other socially meaningful work.

119260.explanation: We support a simple, uniform tax system and general tax reductions, but in the current system, waivers will still be necessary in certain cases.

119263.explanation: Regular public parking spaces may not be removed to create charging spots for electric cars.

119265.explanation: If there are parking problems in neighborhoods, more parking spaces must be created. Parking permits are not a solution but a government instrument to extract even more from motorists and restrict their freedom.

119267.explanation: It is not up to the government to determine which healthcare provider someone must choose. Free choice of care and patient autonomy without government interference are important. The government should not limit people’s free choice of care provider.

119269.explanation: This is not a task of the government.

119270.explanation: We are in favor of a structural reform of the tax system and oppose further increases in taxes for homeowners.

119272.explanation: Tourists are important for Middelburg, and we do not want to chase them away with higher taxes.

119273.explanation: More space is needed for businesses in Middelburg.

119274.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs can perfectly well decide for themselves whether they want to open their store on Sunday or not.

119275.explanation: We give priority to our own residents for social rental and affordable home ownership. Priority for recognized refugees (status holders) will be abolished.

119278.explanation: The swimming pool must be well maintained, and we support an outdoor pool to make the swimming pool more attractive.

119281.explanation: Coffeeshops must be able to legally purchase, supply, and sell. Regulation of production and sales through a permit system with controls takes coffeeshops out of the tolerance zone and disempowers the criminal circuit.

119283.explanation: We do not build massive apartment blocks or towers that affect the livability or character of Middelburg.

119286.explanation: We oppose reducing the number of parking spaces in the city center.

119288.explanation: A legal sex business can limit illegal prostitution. Exploitation and human trafficking must be combated, like other forms of crime.

119289.explanation: There is room for more housing, and the government should not make building impossible by imposing even more rules on the housing sector.

119290.explanation: Of course, community money must be spent carefully, but there are good plans to restore Mill De Hoop in St. Laurens, and we support those plans.

statements.title: Middelburg should allow the establishment of a legal sex business.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
The residents of Middelburg should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Middelburg moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Middelburg should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Middelburg moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: Middelburg should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Middelburg.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: More high-rise residential buildings should be permitted in Middelburg.
The BOAs who patrol for safety in Middelburg should be equipped with batons.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Middelburg controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Middelburg should build a new residential neighborhood on the east side of the N57, along the Canal through Walcheren.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
More business parks should be developed in Middelburg.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The use of open fireplaces and wood-burning stoves should remain permitted in Middelburg.

titleParts.text: In Middelburg moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Middelburg should renovate the Feel Fit Center swimming pool.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the center of Middelburg.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
Middelburg should not allocate funds for the restoration of De Hoop mill in Sint Laurens.
